summary: Fighter-system slice split out from a larger matching-improvement branch (#2617), limited to src/melee/ft. The patch made small matching-oriented rewrites across common fighter, Kirby Yoshi egg, Nana init, collision, and material code: removing temporary locals, reordering declarations and stack padding, replacing M2C/raw access with typed struct fields, inlining or expanding helper logic where it matched better, adding static material TEV data, and correcting a few argument/value expressions. The PR body reports the unsplit source branch had +11,944 matched bytes, 26 new matches, 79 improvements in unmatched items, and no regressions before the directory split; this slice itself was created from origin/master and passed diff-check.
what_changed: Split matching improvements for src/melee/ft out of a larger branch so the fighter subsystem changes could be reviewed independently.

In ftCo_0A01.c, removed a temporary pointer to fp->co_attrs.grav and used fp->co_attrs.grav directly in trajectory math; replaced repeated CPU command emission in ftCo_800AB224 with ftCo_800AD54C_inline0(fp); replaced local dx/dy/sqrtf distance logic and an M2C_FIELD access with ftCo_800A1AB4(fp, target) and typed Fighter_804D64FC->x20[fp->kind].

In ftCo_Attack100.c, adjusted declaration order and initialization timing in ftCo_800D730C, added extern f32 ftCo_804D9018, used it for vel.z instead of literal 0.0f, changed Link catch attrs typing from struct ftLk_DatAttrs* to ftCo_LinkCatchAttrs*, and passed fp->fv.lk.xC to it_802A2B10 instead of the local item variable.

In ftCo_DamageIce.c, expanded JObjRotMtx-style behavior inside ftCo_DamageIce_Init: explicitly obtains y/x rotations, selects HSD_MkRotationMtx versus HSD_MtxQuat based on JOBJ_USE_QUATERNION, and transposes into sp11C/sp14C. The stack matrix and quaternion locals were reordered/introduced for matching.
In ftCo_DownBound.c, removed a local HSD_JObj* temporary and passed fp->parts[FtPart_TopN].joint directly to efAsync_Spawn.
In ftCo_Guard.c, added/adjusted PAD_STACK usage, changed gobj->user_data access to GET_FIGHTER(gobj), and adjusted GuardReflect animation stack padding from 16 to 12.
In ftkirbyyoshiegg.c, introduced a ftCo_DatAttrs_xBC_t* local for fp->co_attrs.xBC, added PAD_STACK(16), and loaded hurtbox offsets/scale through that local.
In ftNn_Init.c, wrapped ftCommon_8007D5D4 through an extra Fighter* fp2 local inside a block, a typical register/lifetime shaping change for matching.

In ftcoll.c, corrected pl_8003E17C argument ordering to fp->player_id, fp->x221F_b4, item->entity; reordered a scaled_dmg declaration; and changed a float-zero comparison to boolean-style !fp->dmg.x189C_unk_num_frames.
In ftmaterial.c, added static HSD_TevDesc ftMaterial_803C69D0 and static HSD_TECnst ftMaterial_803C6A44 data definitions before the force_active region.
smart_moves: Kept the PR as a path-limited fighter slice from a larger branch, reducing review scope and isolating matching deltas to src/melee/ft.
Preferred typed accesses such as Fighter_804D64FC->x20[fp->kind] and ftCo_LinkCatchAttrs* over M2C_FIELD or less-specific struct names, improving readability while preserving matching intent.
Used existing helper logic where it matched better, notably ftCo_800A1AB4 for target distance and ftCo_800AD54C_inline0 for a repeated CPU command sequence.
Used declaration ordering, delayed initialization, PAD_STACK adjustments, direct-vs-temporary field access, and extra scoped locals as targeted codegen controls rather than broad rewrites.
Added explicit material TEV constant data as named static objects, moving unidentified data toward typed C definitions.
decomp_lessons: For fighter matching, small local-variable lifetime changes can matter: moving GET_FIGHTER and fp->x2D0 initialization after declarations, introducing fp2, or replacing a temporary pointer with direct field access may shift register allocation enough to match.
When replacing decompiler artifacts, prefer semantically named project helpers and typed globals over raw calculations or M2C_FIELD macros if the generated code remains matched.
Stack padding should be tuned precisely; this slice includes both added PAD_STACK(8)/PAD_STACK(16) and changed PAD_STACK(16) to PAD_STACK(12).
Expanding a helper inline can be useful when the caller needs exact stack/local layout, as shown by replacing JObjRotMtx calls in DamageIce init with explicit rotation/quaternion matrix construction.
Data matching can be improved by defining static HSD structures directly in C, but the exact initializer layout matters for HSD_TevDesc/HSD_TECnst.

Be cautious with apparent semantic cleanups in matching PRs: changes like argument order in pl_8003E17C and passing fp->fv.lk.xC to it_802A2B10 look meaningful, but the PR evidence does not include reviewer discussion explaining intent.
naming_conventions: Fighter common files retain ftCo_ prefixes and address-like symbol names where functions remain unnamed, e.g. ftCo_800A9CB4, ftCo_800D730C, ftCo_80093BC0.
Inline helper names use the project’s current convention with suffixes such as ftCo_800AD54C_inline0 and ftPp_SpecialS_0_Coll_inline3.
Typed fighter substructures use names such as Fighter_x1A88_t, Fighter_x2D0_t, ftCo_LinkCatchAttrs, and ftCo_DatAttrs_xBC_t rather than raw offsets where known.
New static material data follows address-derived names: ftMaterial_803C69D0 and ftMaterial_803C6A44.
Project macros/helpers such as GET_FIGHTER(gobj), FtPart_TopN, GA_Ground, and JOBJ_USE_QUATERNION are preferred over manual user_data or raw flag usage when they match.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Declaration reordering and delayed assignment to influence register allocation and stack layout.
PAD_STACK insertion and size adjustment to match stack frame expectations.
Replacing local temporaries with direct field expressions, and conversely introducing locals such as xBCp or fp2, depending on the needed codegen.
Using an extern float symbol ftCo_804D9018 instead of literal 0.0f for a vector z component, likely to match a loaded constant/global pattern.
Replacing M2C_FIELD(Fighter_804D64FC, float**, 0x20)[fp->kind] with typed Fighter_804D64FC->x20[fp->kind].
Expanding rotation-matrix logic to explicitly call HSD_JObjGetRotation, HSD_MkRotationMtx/HSD_MtxQuat, and PSMTXTranspose, shaping both calls and local stack objects.
Inlining direct struct member access into call arguments, such as fp->parts[FtPart_TopN].joint in efAsync_Spawn.
Boolean-style float zero test !fp->dmg.x189C_unk_num_frames used instead of == 0.0f where that better matched output.
follow_up_queries: Look for the parent PR or branch #2617 to understand the full matching-improvement context and whether this fighter slice had a measured per-PR match delta.
Search for later PRs touching ftMaterial_803C69D0 or ftMaterial_803C6A44 to see if these static data names or initializers were refined.
Search for uses of ftCo_804D9018 to confirm whether it is a shared zero constant or a meaningful fighter common data symbol.
Search for subsequent cleanup of ftCo_DamageIce_Init to see whether the expanded rotation matrix logic was later factored into a reusable helper without losing the match.
Search for pl_8003E17C call sites to verify the argument order convention established or corrected here.
